page: Vultr vcg-a40-2c-10g-4vram is a fractional GPU instance belonging to the Cloud GPU family. It features 2 vCPUs powered by an Intel Broadwell processor clocked at 2.0 GHz with a shared CPU allocation. The instance is equipped with 10.0 GB of system memory, providing 5.0 GB per core. For acceleration, it includes a fractional NVIDIA A40 GPU with a 0.0833 allocation from the Ampere architecture family, delivering 4 GB of dedicated VRAM. Local storage is provided by a 180 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark results show a mix of average and weak performance across general compute and memory tasks, contrasted by strong performance in specific AI workloads. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance and single-threaded gzip compression both rank in the average tier. However, multi-core CPU performance, Passmark CPU scores, and memory bandwidth are relatively weak, reflecting the shared nature of the 2 vCPUs and the older Broadwell architecture. Conversely, the instance demonstrates strong performance in LLM inference prompt processing, achieving 3480.67 tokens per second for a small 135M model and 202.17 tokens per second for a medium 7B model. Text generation for the 135M model remains average at 114.49 tokens per second.

This instance offers a cost-efficient entry point for developers requiring GPU acceleration without the expense of a dedicated physical GPU. By partitioning the NVIDIA A40 GPU and utilizing shared Intel vCPUs, Vultr provides a low-cost environment for lightweight workloads. The instance is best suited for small-scale LLM inference prompt processing, model prototyping, GPU-accelerated application development, and lightweight machine learning tasks that fit within the 4 GB VRAM limit. It is less suitable for heavy database operations or high-throughput multi-threaded CPU tasks due to its weak multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmarks.
description: Vultr vcg-a40-2c-10g-4vram is a fractional GPU instance designed for entry-level accelerated workloads. It features 2 shared Intel Broadwell vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz, 10.0 GB of system memory, and a 180 GB NVMe SSD. GPU acceleration is delivered via a fractional NVIDIA A40 Ampere GPU with 4 GB of VRAM. While general compute, memory bandwidth, and database benchmarks are weak due to the shared CPU architecture, the instance exhibits strong performance in LLM inference prompt processing, reaching 3480.67 tokens per second on small models. This profile makes the instance a cost-effective option for GPU application development, lightweight machine learning prototyping, and small-scale AI inference tasks that do not require a full dedicated GPU.
og_description: Vultr vcg-a40-2c-10g-4vram is a fractional GPU instance featuring 2 shared Intel vCPUs, 10 GB RAM, a 180 GB NVMe SSD, and a fractional NVIDIA A40 GPU with 4 GB VRAM for entry-level AI workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vcg-a40-2c-10g-4vram is a fractional GPU instance with 2 shared Intel vCPUs, 10 GB RAM, and a 4 GB NVIDIA A40 GPU for lightweight AI tasks.
tagline: A fractional GPU instance combining shared Intel processors with NVIDIA Ampere acceleration for budget-friendly AI prototyping and inference.
bullet_points: Fractional NVIDIA A40 GPU with 4 GB VRAM for budget-friendly acceleration.
2 shared Intel Broadwell vCPUs and 10.0 GB of system memory.
180 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
Strong performance in LLM inference prompt processing for small and medium models.
Best suited for lightweight AI prototyping, development, and small-scale inference.
